title: Sarga 82
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “Having understood the sage's words, Rama approached a sacred lake, frequented by groups of Apsaras, to perform his twilight prayers.” and develops toward “O best of Raghus, you are such a purifier of all embodied beings, and even those on earth who merely speak of you, O scion of Raghu, will attain…”

This passage begins with “Go forth calmly on an auspicious and fearless path, and rule your kingdom with righteousness, for you are indeed the support of the world.” and develops toward “Rama then spoke to the doorkeeper, who was stationed in an inner chamber, instructing him to swiftly go and inform Lakshmana and Bharata of his…”

verses.sanskrit: ऋषेर्वचनमाज्ञाय रामः सन्ध्यामुपासितुम् । उपाक्रमत्सरः पुण्यमप्सरोगणसेवितम् ।। 7.82.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having understood the sage's words, Rama approached a sacred lake, frequented by groups of Apsaras, to perform his twilight prayers.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रोदकमुपस्पृश्य सन्ध्यामन्वास्य पश्चिमाम् । आश्रमं प्राविशद्रामः कुम्भयोनेर्महात्मनः ।। 7.82.2 ।।
verses.translation: There, having performed ablutions and completed the evening twilight prayers, Rama entered the hermitage of the great-souled Agastya.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यागस्त्यो बहुगुणं कन्दमूलं तथौषधिम् । शाल्यादीनि पवित्राणि भोजनार्थमकल्पयत् ।। 7.82.3 ।।
verses.translation: For Rama, Agastya arranged a meal consisting of excellent roots, fruits, herbs, and pure rice and other grains.

verses.sanskrit: स भुक्तवान्नरश्रेष्ठस्तदन्नममृतोपमम् । प्रीतश्च परितुष्टश्च तां रात्रिं समुपाविशत् ।। 7.82.4 ।।
verses.translation: The best among men, Rama, ate that nectar-like food, and being pleased and fully satisfied, he rested for that night.

verses.sanskrit: प्रभाते काल्यमुत्थाय कृत्वा ऽ ऽह्निकमरिन्दमः । ऋषिं समुपचक्राम गमनाय रघूत्तमः ।। 7.82.5 ।।
verses.translation: In the morning, having risen and performed his daily rituals, Rama, the subduer of enemies and best of the Raghu dynasty, approached the sage for his departure.

verses.sanskrit: अभिवाद्याब्रवीद्रामो महर्षिं कुम्भसम्भवम् । आपृच्छे स्वां पुरीं गन्तुं मामनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ।। 7.82.6 ।।
verses.translation: Having saluted the great sage Agastya, Rama spoke, saying, "I ask your leave to go to my own city, and you ought to permit me."

verses.sanskrit: धन्यो ऽस्म्यनुगृहीतो ऽस्मि दर्शनेन महात्मनः । द्रष्टुं चैवागमिष्यामि पावनार्थमिहात्मनः ।। 7.82.7 ।।
verses.translation: I am blessed and favored by the sight of your great self, and I shall indeed come here again to see you for the purification of my own soul.

verses.sanskrit: तथा वदति काकुत्स्थे वाक्यमद्भुतदर्शनम् । उवाच परमप्रीतो धर्मनेत्रस्तपोधनः ।। 7.82.8 ।।
verses.translation: As Rama, whose words were of wonderful meaning, spoke thus, the sage Agastya, whose vision was righteousness and whose wealth was austerity, spoke, being extremely pleased.

verses.sanskrit: अत्यद्भुतमिदं वाक्यं तव राम शुभाक्षरम् । पावनः सर्वभूतानां त्वमेव रघुनन्दन ।। 7.82.9 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, your words are indeed very wonderful and auspicious, for you alone, O delight of the Raghus, are the purifier of all beings.

verses.sanskrit: मुहूर्तमपि राम त्वां ये च पश्यन्ति केचन । पाविताः स्वर्गभूताश्च पूज्यास्ते सर्वदैवतैः ।। 7.82.10 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, even those who merely see you for a moment become purified and attain heaven, and they are then worshipable by all deities.

verses.sanskrit: ये च त्वां घोरचक्षुर्भिः पश्यन्ति प्राणिनो भुवि । हतास्ते यमदण्डेन सद्यो निरयगामिनः ।। 7.82.11 ।।
verses.translation: Those living beings on earth who look at you with hostile eyes are immediately struck down by the rod of Yama and go to hell.

verses.sanskrit: ईदृशस्त्वं रघुश्रेष्ठ पावनः सर्वदेहिनाम् । भुवि त्वां कथयन्तो ऽपि सिद्धिमेष्यन्ति राघव ।। 7.82.12 ।।
verses.translation: O best of Raghus, you are such a purifier of all embodied beings, and even those on earth who merely speak of you, O scion of Raghu, will attain perfection.

verses.sanskrit: गच्छ चारिष्टमव्याग्रः पन्थानमकुतोभयम् । प्रशाधि राज्यं धर्मेण गतिर्हि जगतो भवान् ।। 7.82.13 ।।
verses.translation: Go forth calmly on an auspicious and fearless path, and rule your kingdom with righteousness, for you are indeed the support of the world.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु मुनिना प्राञ्जलिः प्रग्रहो नृपः । अभ्यवादयत प्राज्ञस्तमृषिं पुण्यशालिनम् ।। 7.82.14 ।।
verses.translation: Thus addressed by the sage, the wise and humble king, with folded hands, saluted that virtuous sage.

verses.sanskrit: अभिवाद्य मुनिश्रेष्ठं तांश्च सर्वांस्तपोधनान् । अध्यारोहत्तदव्यग्रः पुष्पकं हेमभूषितम् ।। 7.82.15 ।।
verses.translation: Having saluted the best of sages and all those ascetics, he then calmly ascended the Pushpaka chariot, which was adorned with gold.

verses.sanskrit: तं प्रयान्तं मुनिगणा ह्याशीर्वादैः समन्ततः । अपूजयन्महेन्द्राभं सहस्राक्षमिवामराः ।। 7.82.16 ।।
verses.translation: As Rama departed, groups of sages honored him from all sides with blessings, just as the gods worship Indra, who resembles Mahendra and has a thousand eyes.

verses.sanskrit: स्वस्थः स ददृशे रामः पुष्पके हेमभूषिते । शशी मेघसमीपस्थो यथा जलधरागमे ।। 7.82.17 ।।
verses.translation: Rama, at ease in the gold-adorned Pushpaka, appeared like the moon near a cloud at the onset of the monsoon season.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ऽर्धदिवसे प्राप्ते पूज्यमानस्ततस्ततः । अयोध्यां प्राप्य काकुत्स्थो मध्यकक्ष्यामवारुहत् ।। 7.82.18 ।।
verses.translation: Then, by midday, Rama, being honored from all sides, reached Ayodhya and descended into the middle chamber of his palace.

verses.sanskrit: ततो विसृज्य रुचिरं पुष्पकं कामगामि तत् । विसर्जयित्वा गच्छेति स्वस्ति ते ऽस्त्विति च प्रभुः ।। 7.82.19 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the lord Rama dismissed that beautiful Pushpaka, which moved at will, saying "Go, and let there be welfare for you."

verses.sanskrit: कक्षान्तरविनिक्षिप्तं द्वास्स्थं रामो ऽब्रवीद्वचः । लक्ष्मणं भरतं चैव गत्वा तौ लघुविक्रमौ ।। ममागमनमाख्याय शब्दापयत मा चिरम् ।। 7.82.20 ।।
verses.translation: Rama then spoke to the doorkeeper, who was stationed in an inner chamber, instructing him to swiftly go and inform Lakshmana and Bharata of his arrival, and to summon them without delay. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे व्द्यशीतितमः सर्गः ।। 82 ।। Thus ends the eighty-second sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
